message:
feat(api): add SpeedScore REST endpoints with history and benchmark triggers

Implements REST API endpoints for SpeedScore queries and benchmark
management, enabling the web dashboard to display worker performance
metrics and trigger on-demand benchmarks.

New API endpoints:
- SPEEDSCORE <worker_id>: Get latest SpeedScore for a worker
  Response: { worker_id, speedscore: { total, cpu_score, memory_score,
    disk_score, network_score, compilation_score, measured_at, version } }

- SPEEDSCORE_HISTORY <worker_id> <days> <limit> <offset>: Paginated history
  Response: { worker_id, history: [...], pagination: { total, offset,
    limit, has_more } }

- SPEEDSCORES: List all workers with their latest SpeedScores
  Response: { workers: [{ worker_id, speedscore, status }] }

- BENCHMARK_TRIGGER <worker_id>: Request benchmark execution
  Response: { status, worker_id, request_id }
  Rate-limited with retry_after guidance on 429

- TELEMETRY_POLL <worker_id>: Force telemetry refresh via SSH
- EVENTS: Subscribe to event stream (for WebSocket bridge)

Response types:
- SpeedScoreView: API-friendly SpeedScore representation
- SpeedScoreResponse: Single worker response with optional message
- SpeedScoreHistoryResponse: Paginated history with PaginationInfo
- SpeedScoreListResponse: All workers summary
- BenchmarkTriggerResponse: Trigger confirmation with request_id
- ErrorResponse: Standardized error with optional retry_after_secs

API design principles:
- RESTful naming (noun-based endpoints)
- Consistent error responses with actionable guidance
- Rate limit headers for benchmark triggers
- Optional fields with skip_serializing_if

Implements bead remote_compilation_helper-y8n (SpeedScore REST/WebSocket
API Endpoints) which was blocked on telemetry infrastructure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
